top_issues.title: DQ200 DSG gearbox mechatronics failure
top_issues.description: The 7-speed dry-clutch DQ200 automatic gearbox fitted to many TSI and TDI models is prone to mechatronics unit failure, typically between 16,000 and 42,000 miles. When it fails, the gearbox loses drive or refuses to select gears. Repair costs are substantial and Volkswagen's goodwill support has been inconsistent.
typical_symptoms: Loss of drive or inability to select gears
Gearbox error message on dashboard
Car stuck in neutral or one gear
Hesitation or jerking on take-off
Limp-home mode activation

top_issues.title: Leaking and failing shock absorbers (dampers)
top_issues.description: Front and rear dampers on many Golfs develop oil leaks or fail prematurely, often within the first few years or before 50,000 miles. The problem is particularly common on models with sports suspension. Dealers have sometimes claimed this is a characteristic of driving style rather than a defect, and warranty support has been limited.
typical_symptoms: Oil weeping or misting visible on damper body
Rattling noise from rear suspension over bumps
Poor ride quality or excessive bounce
Visible oil stains on wheel or suspension arm

top_issues.title: Electromechanical parking brake malfunction
top_issues.description: The electronic parking brake can fail to release, release unexpectedly, or seize on. Some owners report the car rolling away on hills when the brake fails to hold, whilst others experience the brake refusing to disengage when trying to drive away. The fault is often intermittent and difficult for dealers to diagnose.
typical_symptoms: Brake fails to release when trying to drive away
Brake releases unexpectedly when parked
Car rolls forward or backward on a slope
Red warning light on dashboard
Whirring sound absent when brake is applied

top_issues.title: Adaptive cruise control (ACC) sensor faults and autonomous braking
top_issues.description: The ACC radar sensor can fail, become misaligned, or trigger unwanted emergency braking. Some owners report the car braking hard for no reason, whilst others experience the system disabling without warning. Recalibration is expensive and not always covered by warranty. The sensor is also vulnerable to theft.
typical_symptoms: ACC disabled warning message
Sudden hard braking with no obstacle ahead
ACC sensor stolen or missing
No forward view message displayed
ACC system fails to engage or disengage

top_issues.title: Infotainment system (MMI) failure
top_issues.description: The infotainment system can fail to power on or off, lose radio and sat-nav functionality, or become completely unresponsive. Dealers have reported being unable to fix the problem, with Volkswagen stating that replacement units do not resolve the issue and no software update is available.
typical_symptoms: Infotainment screen will not turn on or off
Radio, sat-nav, or phone integration stops working
Loss of access to vehicle menus
Screen goes blank or freezes
No response to button presses on head unit

overall_reliability_note: The Mk7 Golf is generally a solid and well-built family hatchback, but the DQ200 DSG gearbox, electromechanical parking brake, and ACC system have proven troublesome on many examples, particularly early production cars from 2013–2016. Diesel variants require careful maintenance to avoid DPF and turbo issues, and damper leaks are common.
